etymology.description: 是(是) (shì, "to be; yes; this; correct") comes from the noonday sun (日) directly overhead as the natural standard of straightness/uprightness (正 "straight"). What is upright is what is correct -> 'right, correct' -> what is correct is what 'is so' -> affirmation 'yes' and the copula 'to be'. 嗎(嗎) (ma, "question particle") is an interrogative particle used at the end of sentences to turn them into questions.
etymology.interpretation: 是(是) (shì, "to be; yes") carries the idea of affirmation or agreement, connecting to the core sense of correctness or truth. When combined with 嗎(嗎) (ma, "question particle"), which turns statements into questions, it invites confirmation or seeks verification, hence forming the phrase "really?; is that so?" where one seeks reassurance or truth of a matter.

examples.chinese: 你今天没去上班(你今天沒去上班)？是吗(是嗎)？
examples.pinyin: Nǐ jīntiān méi qù shàngbān? Shì ma?
examples.english: You didn't go to work today? Really?

examples.chinese: 他是你的兄弟(他是你的兄弟)？是吗(是嗎)？
examples.pinyin: Tā shì nǐ de xiōngdì? Shì ma?
examples.english: He is your brother? Is that so?

examples.chinese: 她已经毕业了(她已經畢業了)？是吗(是嗎)？
examples.pinyin: Tā yǐjīng bìyè le? Shì ma?
